perturbation.original_text: A Party fails to make, when due, any payment under this JSMA and such failure is not remedied within three (3) Business Days after written notice of such failure is given to the Party
perturbation.changed_text: A Party fails to make, when due, any payment under this JSMA and such failure is not remedied within one (1) Business Days after written notice of such failure is given to the Party
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the cure period for payment failure to one business day may be considered commercially unreasonable, especially considering the potential for banking delays and other unforeseen circumstances. This could conflict with principles of contract law requiring good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Principles of Good Faith and Fair Dealing in Contract Law
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 1-304 imposes an obligation of good faith in the performance and enforcement of every contract. While Alabama has adopted the UCC, reducing the cure period to a single business day may be interpreted as a violation of the duty of good faith, as it unreasonably restricts the defaulting party's ability to rectify the payment failure due to circumstances beyond their immediate control. This is particularly true in transactions involving substantial sums of money and international parties.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The change reduces the cure period for payment failure from three to one business day. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation argue that this reduction could violate the duty of good faith under UCC § 1-304, especially if circumstances beyond the defaulting party's control prevent them from rectifying the payment in one day. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that "Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement." By reducing the cure period to one day, the modified text creates a time-sensitive obligation that may not allow for good faith performance, thus creating a contradiction because the change weakens the obligation of good faith.

perturbation.original_text: The obligation of confidentiality shall be of a continuing nature for 2 years after termination of this JSMA and shall not be canceled by the expiration, suspension or termination of the JSMA.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation of confidentiality shall be of a continuing nature for 10 years after termination of this JSMA and shall not be canceled by the expiration, suspension or termination of the JSMA.
perturbation.explanation: Extending the confidentiality obligation to 10 years may be deemed an unreasonable restraint of trade under Alabama law, particularly if the information covered is not a trade secret or if the duration is disproportionate to the competitive advantage gained.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Alabama law regarding Restraint of Trade
perturbation.law_explanation: Alabama Code § 8-1-1 generally prohibits contracts that restrain trade. While reasonable restrictions may be permissible, a 10-year confidentiality obligation, particularly if it covers information that is not genuinely a trade secret or that becomes outdated quickly in the dynamic bunker fuel market, could be viewed as an excessive and unenforceable restraint on competition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the confidentiality obligation from 2 years to 10 years. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation suggest this could be an unreasonable restraint of trade under Alabama law, particularly if the information is not a trade secret or if the duration is disproportionate. Even without scraped snippets, the provided explanations clearly establish that the longer duration could create a legal contradiction as it might violate Alabama's prohibition on contracts that restrain trade. This aligns with the Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction definition, where time-sensitive obligations (the duration of confidentiality) do not align with legal requirements, potentially making the terms unenforceable.
